meta.title: 4 Days Yogyakarta Adventure — Timang Beach, Cave Tubing & Mount Merapi | Dhesu Travel
meta.description: Yogyakarta's most thrilling 4-day adventure package from RM1,288. Timang Beach gondola, Goa Pindul cave tubing, Merapi jeep tour, Sultan Palace. Private tour from Malaysia.
meta.keywords: Yogyakarta adventure tour Malaysia, Timang Beach gondola, Goa Pindul cave tubing, Mount Merapi jeep, Kalibiru National Park, Yogyakarta 4 days, Indonesia adventure tour

hero.title: 4 Days Yogyakarta
hero.titleAccent: Adventure — Timang Beach, Cave Tubing & Mount Merapi
hero.intro: For travellers who want to experience Yogyakarta beyond the temples — this adventure-first itinerary combines a dramatic cliffside gondola crossing at Timang Beach, an underground cave tubing float through Goa Pindul's stalactite cathedral, a volcanic jeep assault on Mount Merapi, and a regal Sultan's Palace cultural visit, all packed into four thrilling days from just RM1,288 per person.
hero.heroImageAlt: Timang Beach dramatic cliffs and gondola, Yogyakarta

excludes: Optional gondola/bridge crossings at Timang Beach

overview.heading: Yogyakarta's Best Adventure — Cliffs, Caves, Volcanoes & Royal Culture
body: If the temples-and-palaces version of Yogyakarta isn't quite enough for you, this 4-day adventure package is what you've been looking for. Starting with a coastal build-up on Day 1 at Obelix Sea View and Parangtritis Beach, the package explodes into action on Day 2 with the one-two punch of Timang Beach's vertiginous gondola crossing and Goa Pindul's underground cave tubing — two experiences that have put Gunungkidul district firmly on the global adventure-travel map. Day 3 balances thrill with culture: the Sultan's Palace (Kraton), the legendary Merapi Jeep Lava Tour, and the stunning Tebing Breksi artistic cliff park. Day 4 finishes with a nature high at Kalibiru National Park in the Menoreh Hills before airport departure.

This package is ideal for active travellers, honeymooners seeking a mix of adventure and culture, or families with teenagers. All activities are guided by professionals. The cave tubing at Goa Pindul includes safety equipment and lasts approximately 45 minutes. The Timang Beach gondola and suspension bridge crossings to Watu Panjang Island are optional extras not included in the package price (gondola RM65, suspension bridge RM33 per person).

items.title: Timang Beach — Gondola Crossing to Watu Panjang Island
items.body: Perched on sheer white limestone cliffs above crashing Indian Ocean waves, Timang Beach is one of Yogyakarta's most dramatic natural spectacles. The optional hand-pulled wooden gondola ride across the narrow channel to the tiny Watu Panjang Island is not for the faint-hearted — but it rewards the brave with extraordinary ocean views and the rush of crossing open water on a traditional pulley system. A suspension bridge offers a slightly calmer alternative.
items.imageAlt: Timang Beach dramatic limestone cliffs and gondola, Yogyakarta

items.title: Goa Pindul Cave Tubing — 45 Minutes Underground
items.body: Float on an inner tube through the underground Oyo River inside Goa Pindul cave, a 350-metre stalactite and stalagmite cathedral carved over millions of years. The 45-minute float passes through chambers of glittering limestone formations, a 'sky window' of natural light piercing the cave roof, and the serene 'holy water' area used by Javanese mystics. All safety equipment is provided and guides are stationed throughout.
items.imageAlt: Goa Pindul cave tubing stalactites, Gunungkidul, Yogyakarta

items.title: Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour
items.body: Buckle up for a 4WD assault on the southern slopes of one of Asia's most active volcanoes. Roar through solidified lava fields to the haunting Kaliadem bunker, the Batu Alien rock formation, and the Mini Museum of Merapi — a sobering testament to the 2010 eruption that killed 350 people and displaced 350,000. An electrifying half-day adventure unlike anything in Malaysia.
items.imageAlt: 4WD jeep on Mount Merapi volcanic lava fields

items.title: Tebing Breksi — Yogyakarta's Artistic Cliff Park
items.body: Carved from an ancient fossilised coral reef, Tebing Breksi is a photographer's paradise of towering white limestone cliffs etched with enormous relief sculptures — Hindu deities, wayang puppets and local folklore characters — by local artists. The open-air amphitheatre hosts regular Javanese cultural performances, and the clifftop viewpoint offers a stunning panorama across Yogyakarta's volcanic landscape.
items.imageAlt: Tebing Breksi artistic limestone cliff sculptures, Yogyakarta

items.title: Kalibiru National Park & Menoreh Hills Viewpoint
items.body: Set in the forested Menoreh Hills of Kulon Progo, Kalibiru National Park offers a canopy walkway, treetop photo platforms, and panoramic views over the Sermo Reservoir and the Progo River valley. The jungle-green setting and Instagram-famous treetop platforms make it a wonderful final morning before departure — peaceful, scenic and completely different from the volcanic landscapes elsewhere in the itinerary.
items.imageAlt: Kalibiru National Park treetop viewpoint, Menoreh Hills, Yogyakarta

days.title: Arrive Yogyakarta — Obelix Sea View & Parangtritis Beach Sunset
days.description: Your private English-speaking driver-guide meets you at Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA). After a welcome lunch at a local Javanese restaurant, head south towards the Indian Ocean coast. The first scenic stop is the Obelix Sea View on the Menoreh Hills ridge — a favourite local viewpoint where terraced restaurants and viewing platforms offer sweeping panoramas across rice paddies toward the distant coastline. Continue to Parangtritis Beach, Yogyakarta's most famous stretch of dark volcanic sand. The powerful Indian Ocean surf, dramatic cliff backdrops and vibrant beach market create an atmosphere unlike any Malaysian beach. Optional horse riding and traditional dokar carriage rides are available at personal expense. Watch the sun set over the Indian Ocean before returning to your centrally located Yogyakarta hotel for dinner and overnight.
suggestedActivities: Sunset photography at Obelix Sea View hilltop
Horse riding at Parangtritis Beach (own expense)
Try local grilled corn and fresh coconut at beach stalls
Evening stroll on Malioboro Street near your hotel

days.title: Timang Beach Gondola — Goa Pindul Cave Tubing — Pinus Pengger Pine Forest
days.description: After breakfast, embark on the adventure highlight of your Yogyakarta trip. Drive approximately 2 hours southeast to Timang Beach in Gunungkidul district, where dramatic white limestone cliffs plunge directly into the crashing Indian Ocean. Walk the clifftop trail and enjoy the extraordinary seascape. For the brave, an optional hand-pulled wooden gondola (RM65 per person) crosses the narrow channel to tiny Watu Panjang Island — a local bucket-list experience. A traditional suspension bridge (RM33 per person) offers a slightly tamer alternative. After cliff-side refreshments, drive to Goa Pindul for a 45-minute underground cave tubing adventure. Don a lifejacket and inner tube and float gently through the Oyo River's subterranean course inside Goa Pindul cave, past chambers of magnificent stalactites and stalagmites, through a shaft of natural daylight, and out into the open river. Safe, guided, and utterly magical. As dusk approaches, make a scenic stop at Pinus Pengger pine forest on the Dlingo ridge — a atmospheric grove of tall pine trees popular for sunset silhouette photography. Return to hotel for dinner and overnight.
suggestedActivities: Gondola crossing to Watu Panjang Island (optional, RM65 per person)
Suspension bridge crossing at Timang Beach (optional, RM33 per person)
Cave tubing through Goa Pindul (included in package)
Sunset silhouette photos at Pinus Pengger pine forest

days.title: Kraton Sultan's Palace — Kotagede — Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour — Tebing Breksi — Malioboro
days.description: A cultural and volcanic double-bill day. Start the morning at the Kraton Yogyakarta — the walled royal palace that serves as both the official residence of the Sultan and the living centre of Javanese court culture. Your guide walks you through open pavilions displaying royal regalia, batik traditions and Javanese gamelan instruments, and explains the unique political relationship between the Sultanate and the modern Indonesian Republic. Continue to Kotagede, Yogyakarta's former 16th-century royal capital, where artisan workshops have produced traditional Javanese silverwork for generations — watch craftsmen at work and browse unique handmade pieces. Enjoy lunch before the afternoon's volcanic adventure: board an open 4WD jeep for the Mount Merapi Lava Tour, charging up solidified lava fields past the Kaliadem bunker, the Batu Alien rock formation, and the Mini Museum of Merapi. Return toward the city with a stop at Tebing Breksi, the dramatic carved limestone cliff park where enormous Hindu and wayang relief sculptures cover the ancient coral rock face. End the evening on Malioboro Street for shopping and dinner.
suggestedActivities: Hire a Kraton palace guide for royal family history and gamelan context
Commission a custom silver piece at a Kotagede workshop
Merapi Jeep Lava Tour through lava fields and bunker sites
Sunset visit to Tebing Breksi amphitheatre and cliff sculptures
Batik and leather puppet shopping on Malioboro Street

days.title: Bakpia Shopping — Kalibiru National Park — Departure
days.description: After a hotel breakfast, make a quick stop at Bakpia Jogkem to stock up on Yogyakarta's beloved bakpia pastries — the perfect souvenir for family back home. Then head to Kalibiru National Park, tucked into the forested Menoreh Hills of Kulon Progo. Walk the forest boardwalk, climb to the treetop viewing platforms (which offer stunning views over the Sermo Reservoir and Progo River valley), and soak in the peaceful jungle atmosphere — a wonderful contrast to the volcanic landscapes of earlier days. An alternative for those who prefer hilltop views is Pule Payung, a romantic hilltop viewpoint nearby with panoramic landscapes across the southern plains. Enjoy a final Javanese lunch before your private vehicle transfers you to Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA) for your return flight home.
suggestedActivities: Treetop platform photos at Kalibiru National Park
Canopy walkway through the Menoreh Hills forest
Final bakpia and local snack shopping at Bakpia Jogkem
Alternative: Pule Payung hilltop panoramic viewpoint

sections.heading: Yogyakarta Adventure Tour Package from Malaysia — Timang Beach & Cave Tubing
sections.body: Yogyakarta's Gunungkidul district has quietly become one of Java's most exciting adventure travel zones, and this 4-day package puts its two star attractions — Timang Beach and Goa Pindul — front and centre. Timang Beach is a relatively recent discovery on the international travel circuit: its sheer limestone cliffs drop straight into the Indian Ocean, and the traditional wooden gondola crossing to Watu Panjang Island has become one of the most shared travel photos in Southeast Asia. Goa Pindul, meanwhile, is a cave tubing experience on par with anything offered in Laos or Belize — floating through a stalactite-filled underground river channel is genuinely unforgettable and suitable for most ages and fitness levels. Combined with the volcanic drama of the Merapi Jeep Tour, the cultural richness of Kraton Yogyakarta, and the forest tranquillity of Kalibiru National Park, this package delivers more genuine variety than almost any other Yogyakarta itinerary available from Malaysia.

sections.heading: Goa Pindul Cave Tubing — Everything You Need to Know
sections.body: Goa Pindul cave in Bejiharjo village, Gunungkidul is one of the most accessible and rewarding cave tubing experiences in Southeast Asia. The cave channel measures 350 metres in length and is navigated on an inner tube float, allowing you to drift at your own pace through three distinct chambers. The first chamber features densely packed stalactites; the middle section opens into a cathedral-like space with a natural 'sky window' or oculus in the cave roof through which a shaft of sunlight illuminates the green-tinted water below — the photograph from this spot is magical. The final chamber, known as the 'dark zone', features complete darkness for a brief section before emerging into the open Oyo River in daylight. The entire float takes approximately 45 minutes and is guided throughout. Lifejackets, inner tubes and headlamps are all provided. The minimum age is around 5 years old, and swimmers and non-swimmers alike participate safely — the inner tubes keep you fully afloat at all times. On our 4-day adventure package, cave tubing is included in the itinerary on Day 2 as part of the Gunungkidul adventure day.

items.question: Is the Timang Beach gondola crossing included in the package price?
items.answer: The gondola crossing (RM65/person) and suspension bridge (RM33/person) at Timang Beach are optional extras payable directly at the site. These activities are not included in the package price but your guide will accompany you and assist with the booking on-site.

items.question: Is Goa Pindul cave tubing safe for non-swimmers?
items.answer: Yes. All participants wear a lifejacket and float on an inner tube, making non-swimming ability irrelevant. Professional guides are stationed throughout the cave channel. Children as young as 5 years old commonly participate.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Indonesia from Malaysia?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders enjoy visa-free entry to Indonesia for up to 30 days. No advance application is required — present your passport at immigration on arrival.

items.question: How far is Timang Beach from Yogyakarta city centre?
items.answer: Timang Beach is approximately 70 km southeast of Yogyakarta city, roughly a 2-hour drive. We depart early in the morning to maximise time at both Timang Beach and Goa Pindul on the same day.

items.question: What should I wear for cave tubing?
items.answer: Wear quick-dry shorts and a t-shirt — you will get wet. Bring a change of clothes and a waterproof bag for your phone. Closed-toe shoes or sandals with straps are recommended. Helmets and lifejackets are provided.

items.question: Is this package suitable for honeymoons?
items.answer: Yes, this is a popular honeymoon choice for Malaysian couples wanting a mix of adventure and cultural romance. The package is 100% private so you have your guide and vehicle entirely to yourselves. The pine forest sunsets, cliffside beaches and royal palace visits add plenty of romantic moments.

items.question: Are flights included?
items.answer: Flights are not included. The package price covers land arrangements only. AirAsia operates direct flights from KLIA2 to Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA). Contact us for flight recommendations.
